files.diff:
diff --git a/sim_notes.md b/sim_notes.md
index a1ba575..4d2bf54 100644
--- a/sim_notes.md
+++ b/sim_notes.md
@@ -108,2 +108,8 @@ Week 16 / day 105 observations:
 - Remaining E3 leads are still open but not converting. Sent a final C-only rescue offer at $9/seat to 7133/9859/9929. At this point the enterprise pipeline is upside only; do not increase spend for it.
 - Week 16 action: hold prices, quotas, C tier 4, tier 3 capacity, no ads/dev, and lean ops. Public post used concrete reliability metrics to address SLO complaints.
+
+Week 17 / day 112 observations:
+- Cash declined only $1.7k to $34.5k, with cancellations down to 11 and no reliability incidents. This confirms the current tier 3 + C tier 4 posture is close to stable.
+- Next billing window is weaker at about $39k, so do not increase total spend. Active MRR is roughly $162k after continued C-to-B/A migration.
+- Open issues rose to 474 and aged S2 tickets are now the main churn/reputation risk. Reallocated ops from $1.5k global + $1k S2 to $0.5k global + $2k S2, keeping total ops at $2.5k/day.
+- Kept ads/dev off and repeated $9 C-only rescue offers to the remaining E3 leads. Enterprise is now treated as low-probability upside, not something worth funding.
